---
title: "Build"
description: "Build Go templates synchronously or in the background and inspect build status."
---

# Build

In the Go SDK, template builds are started with package-level helpers on `e2b`, not instance methods on `TemplateBase`.

## Go-specific behavior

- use `e2b.Build(ctx, template, name, opts)` to build and wait for completion
- use `e2b.BuildInBackground(ctx, template, name, opts)` to return after the build is created and triggered
- `BuildOptions` embeds `BasicBuildOptions`, so build-time fields such as `CpuCount`, `MemoryMB`, `SkipCache`, `Tags`, and `OnBuildLogs` live under `BasicBuildOptions: e2b.BasicBuildOptions{...}`
- `GetBuildStatus(...)` accepts a `*e2b.BuildInfo`, not separate `templateID` and `buildID` parameters
- current build statuses are `"building"`, `"waiting"`, `"ready"`, and `"error"`
- the root package re-exports `TemplateBuildStatus`, but not named `BuildStatus*` constants, so compare against typed string values such as `e2b.TemplateBuildStatus("ready")`
- if `OnBuildLogs` is `nil`, the Go SDK does not automatically install `DefaultBuildLogger()`
- if `name == ""`, `BuildOptions.BasicBuildOptions.Alias` is used only as a legacy fallback
- `BuildOptions.BasicBuildOptions.SkipCache` applies to the whole build request; `template.SkipCache()` only affects the next template layer

## Build and wait for completion

`Build(...)` waits until the build reaches `ready` or `error`.

```go
package main

import (
	"context"

	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()
	timeoutMs := 120000

	template := e2b.Template(nil).
		FromBaseImage().
		RunCmd(`echo "hello"`)

	buildInfo, _ := e2b.Build(ctx, template, "my-template", &e2b.BuildOptions{
		BasicBuildOptions: e2b.BasicBuildOptions{
			CpuCount:    2,
			MemoryMB:    2048,
			SkipCache:   false,
			OnBuildLogs: e2b.DefaultBuildLogger(),
		},
		ApiKey:           "your-api-key",
		Domain:           "your-domain",
		RequestTimeoutMs: &timeoutMs,
	})

	if buildInfo != nil {
		_ = buildInfo.Name
		_ = buildInfo.Alias
		_ = buildInfo.Tags
		_ = buildInfo.TemplateID
		_ = buildInfo.BuildID
	}
}
```

`BuildInfo` currently includes `Name`, `Alias`, `Tags`, `TemplateID`, and `BuildID`.

## Build in background

`BuildInBackground(...)` returns as soon as the build is created and the template payload is submitted.

```go
package main

import (
	"context"

	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()
	template := e2b.Template(nil).FromBaseImage()

	buildInfo, _ := e2b.BuildInBackground(ctx, template, "my-template", &e2b.BuildOptions{
		BasicBuildOptions: e2b.BasicBuildOptions{
			CpuCount: 2,
			MemoryMB: 2048,
		},
	})

	if buildInfo != nil {
		_ = buildInfo.TemplateID
		_ = buildInfo.BuildID
	}
}
```

Use this form when you want to poll status yourself or hand the build identifiers to another process.

## Check build status

Use `GetBuildStatus(...)` with the `*e2b.BuildInfo` returned by `BuildInBackground(...)` or `Build(...)`.

```go
package main

import (
	"context"

	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()

	status, _ := e2b.GetBuildStatus(ctx, &e2b.BuildInfo{
		TemplateID: "tmpl_123",
		BuildID:    "bld_123",
	}, &e2b.GetBuildStatusOptions{
		LogsOffset: 0,
	})

	if status != nil {
		_ = status.BuildID
		_ = status.TemplateID
		_ = status.Status
		_ = status.LogEntries
		_ = status.Logs
		if status.Reason != nil {
			_ = status.Reason.Message
			_ = status.Reason.Step
			_ = status.Reason.LogEntries
		}
	}
}
```

Current status values:

- `e2b.TemplateBuildStatus("building")`
- `e2b.TemplateBuildStatus("waiting")`
- `e2b.TemplateBuildStatus("ready")`
- `e2b.TemplateBuildStatus("error")`

## Example: Background build with status polling

When polling manually, keep advancing `LogsOffset` so you only fetch new log entries.

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"time"

	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()
	template := e2b.Template(nil).FromBaseImage()

	buildInfo, err := e2b.BuildInBackground(ctx, template, "my-template", &e2b.BuildOptions{
		BasicBuildOptions: e2b.BasicBuildOptions{
			CpuCount: 2,
			MemoryMB: 2048,
		},
	})
	if err != nil {
		return
	}

	logsOffset := 0

	for {
		buildStatus, err := e2b.GetBuildStatus(ctx, buildInfo, &e2b.GetBuildStatusOptions{
			LogsOffset: logsOffset,
		})
		if err != nil || buildStatus == nil {
			return
		}

		logsOffset += len(buildStatus.LogEntries)

		for _, entry := range buildStatus.LogEntries {
			_ = entry.String()
		}

		switch buildStatus.Status {
		case e2b.TemplateBuildStatus("ready"):
			return
		case e2b.TemplateBuildStatus("error"):
			return
		}

		time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
	}
}
```

Polling code should keep waiting through both `"building"` and `"waiting"` states.

For build-log callback details, see [Logging](/docs/template/logging). For the full build and status API surface, see the [Go SDK Template reference](/docs/sdk-reference/go-sdk/template).
